steps.title: Check for exponential pattern with constant ratio
steps.content:
For exponential functions, consecutive y-values have a constant ratio. For linear functions, consecutive y-values have a constant difference.

Calculate the ratio between consecutive outputs:
$$\frac{f(0)}{f(-1)} = \frac{1}{1/69} = 69$$
$$\frac{f(1)}{f(0)} = \frac{69}{1} = 69$$
$$\frac{f(2)}{f(1)} = \frac{4761}{69} = 69$$
The ratio is constant at 69 across all intervals. This constant multiplicative ratio is the hallmark of an exponential function, ruling out the linear choices A and B.

steps.title: Determine direction of exponential function
steps.content: Since the constant ratio (69) is greater than 1, each step increase in $x$ multiplies $f(x)$ by 69. This creates exponential growth, making the function increase rather than decrease. That matches choice C — C is correct.
